measurements.measurement_reference: Ground floor storefront opening and second-floor window used for scale. ~18 courses per 1.3m window.

brick_analysis.bond_notes: Standard running bond. Only visible on left unit (145) — right unit (147) is painted white, obscuring brick pattern.

colour.description: Medium red-brown brick, slightly muted/darkened — possibly from age, soot, or urban soiling. The brick appears darker/browner than newer-looking buildings in the area. Consistent with older commercial construction. Only the left unit (145) shows exposed brick; right unit (147) is entirely painted white.

special_features.description: Mansard roof profile with dormer windows on both units. Dark green/black shingle covering. The Mansard roof is a distinctive French Second Empire feature suggesting 1870s-1880s construction.
Commercial storefront at ground level with large windows. The storefront framework is timber/metal within the brick opening.
Simple flat brick lintel above the second-floor window.

condition.spalling_locations: Some surface deterioration visible on exposed brick
condition.notes: Two-storey commercial building pair with Mansard roofs. The left unit (145) retains exposed brick; the right (147) is painted white. The Mansard roof form suggests this is among the older commercial buildings on Augusta Ave (1870s-1880s). Utility pole and transformer partially obscure the facade centre. A bench and merchandise display visible at street level — active commercial use.

texture_calibration.notes: Utility pole and transformer create significant central obstruction. Twilight/dusk lighting (blue sky, artificial lights on) makes colour assessment less reliable. The Mansard roof profile is important for the 3D model — it defines the building silhouette.
